Daily DAP Accessibility Report — 2026-03-16#

Run ID: run-2026-03-16-8aa0475fbd · Status: success · Source data: 2026-03-14 · Generated: 2026-03-16T10:30:40.519Z

About These Reports#

The Digital Analytics Program (DAP) is a U.S. government analytics service that collects website traffic data across participating federal agencies. DAP tracks page views, visitor counts, and usage patterns for hundreds of government websites, providing transparency into how the public engages with federal digital services.

This report measures the quality and accessibility of the top 100 most-visited U.S. government URLs as reported by DAP. Each day, Lighthouse scans are run against these URLs to measure:

  • Performance - How fast pages load for users (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Accessibility - How well pages work for users with disabilities, following WCAG guidelines (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Best Practices - Whether pages follow modern web development standards (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • SEO - How well pages are optimized for search engines (scores 0-100, higher is better)

Accessibility findings come from axe-core, the industry-standard accessibility testing engine embedded in Lighthouse. The axe findings surface specific WCAG violations such as missing alternative text, insufficient color contrast, and missing form labels that make government websites harder to use for people with disabilities.

Traffic data reflects daily visitor counts from DAP. URLs are ranked by page load count, ensuring the most-used government pages are prioritized for quality measurement.

Accessibility Trend Narrative#

Over the past 19 days of data (2026-02-26 to 2026-03-16), government website accessibility scores have improved (+23.35 points, from 68 to 91.35). Performance scores have improved (+16.77 points, from 38 to 54.77).

Today's aggregate accessibility score of 91.33 reflects the mean Lighthouse accessibility score across 100 successfully scanned government URLs. A score above 90 indicates generally strong compliance with WCAG automated checks, though manual testing is always recommended to fully assess accessibility.

Common Accessibility Issues (Top 10)#

The following axe-core rules were most frequently violated across scanned URLs today. These patterns indicate systemic accessibility barriers present across multiple government websites.

Top axe-core accessibility rule violations across scanned URLs
Rule ID Description URLs affected Disabilities Affected (hover/focus icons for estimated impact)
target-sizeTouch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.31~462.2K ~1.2M
heading-orderHeading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order26~157.2K ~47.2K ~345.9K
landmark-one-mainDocument does not have a main landmark.25~208.7K ~62.6K ~459.2K
color-contrastBackground and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.24~258.4K ~463.1K
link-nameLinks do not have a discernible name15~142.5K ~42.7K ~313.5K
label-content-name-mismatchElements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names.15~40.8K ~97.9K ~12.2K ~89.8K
image-altImage elements do not have `[alt]` attributes13~74.7K ~22.4K
link-in-text-blockLinks rely on color to be distinguishable.9~39.9K ~71.5K
html-has-lang`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute8~60.9K ~18.3K ~286.4K
button-nameButtons do not have an accessible name8~15.6K ~4.7K
Disability icon key
Without Vision — People who are blind or have no functional vision (1.0% of U.S. population — ~3,400,000 Americans)
Limited Vision — People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast (2.4% of U.S. population — ~8,100,000 Americans)
Without Perception of Color — People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness) (4.3% of U.S. population — ~14,500,000 Americans)
Without Hearing — People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content (0.3% of U.S. population — ~1,100,000 Americans)
Limited Hearing — People with hearing loss who may struggle with audio without accommodations (3.5% of U.S. population — ~11,900,000 Americans)
Without Speech — People who cannot use speech or voice-based input effectively (0.5% of U.S. population — ~1,700,000 Americans)
Limited Manipulation — People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity (2.2% of U.S. population — ~7,600,000 Americans)
Limited Reach and Strength — People with limited reach, strength, or stamina (5.8% of U.S. population — ~19,600,000 Americans)
Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities — People with cognitive, learning, or language differences (4.7% of U.S. population — ~15,900,000 Americans)

These icons show which groups of people with disabilities are excluded by each accessibility barrier. Where page view data is available, each icon shows an estimated number of people potentially excluded (page loads for affected URLs × disability prevalence rate from U.S. Census ACS 2022). Hover over or focus an icon to see the full estimate and methodology. Icons follow the Section 508 Functional Performance Criteria and the equivalent EU EN 301 549 v3.2.1 Table B.2 categories.

Prevalence data: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2022 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101; supplemental data from CDC, NIDCD, AFB, and NIH/NEI. U.S. population base: ~335,900,000 (2022 estimate). Reviewed annually.

See the Section 508 WCAG to FPC mapping for additional detail on how accessibility requirements map to functional needs.

Estimated Impact (daily)#

No accessibility findings data available for this scan. The impact estimation requires detailed accessibility findings from scanning tools. Currently, the scanner is running in a mode that does not collect individual accessibility issues.

Americans Excluded by Disability#

No accessibility findings data is available to estimate excluded users by disability category for this scan.

Performance Impact on Americans#

Google defines a good Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) as under 2.5 seconds and recommends pages under 1.6 MB. The figures below estimate how much extra time Americans spend waiting, and how much extra data is transferred, because government websites fall short of these benchmarks. Calculations are based on 98 successfully scanned URLs with Lighthouse timing data.

Estimated performance impact vs. Google benchmarks for today's scanned government URLs
Metric Estimated total (today) Notes
Extra time waiting (vs 2.5s LCP benchmark) 3,044.1 days 263,010,419 seconds
Extra data transferred (vs 1.6 MB page weight benchmark) 47,369.97 GB Across 98 URLs with weight data

Extra time is calculated as: for each scanned URL, max(0, actual LCP − 2.5s) × page loads. Extra data is calculated as: max(0, actual page weight − 1.6 MB) × page loads. LCP and page weight are measured by Lighthouse. These are rough estimates based on a sample of the top government URLs by traffic.

Accessibility Findings

https://tools.usps.com

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 68

Axe findings: 10

`button`, `link`, and `menuitem` elements do not have accessible names. (rule: aria-command-name)

When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make command elements more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~88.6K ~26.6K ~195.0K

Affected elements (3):

Element 1

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a#headingOneAnchor > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 2

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a.collapsed > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 3

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a.collapsed > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children)

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Disabilities affected: ~88.6K ~26.6K ~195.0K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div#g-navigation > div.nav-full > nav > ul.nav-list

Snippet:

<ul class="nav-list" role="menubar">

Label: Skip Quick Tools Links Quick Tools Track a Package Informed Delivery …

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has children which are not allowed: li[tabindex]
`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent)

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Assistive technologies, like screen readers, can't interpret ARIA attributes with invalid names. Learn more about valid ARIA attributes.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~88.6K ~26.6K ~195.0K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div#accordion > div.panel > div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title

Snippet:

<h4 class="panel-title">

Label: What does my tracking number look like?

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~88.6K ~26.6K ~195.0K

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div#tracking_page_wrapper > div#g-navigation > div.nav-full > a#endnav

Snippet:

<a name="endnav" id="endnav" href="#" class="hidden-skip">

Label:  

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

https://forecast.weather.gov

Axe findings: 5

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Element 1

Element path: div.one-sixth-first > div#forecast-lookup > form#getForecast > input#inputstring

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~23.6K ~7.1K ~110.7K

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://informeddelivery.usps.com

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 71

Axe findings: 9

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children)

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Disabilities affected: ~15.9K ~4.8K ~35.1K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div#g-navigation > div.nav-full > nav > ul.nav-list

Snippet:

<ul class="nav-list" role="menubar">

Label: Skip Quick Tools Links Quick Tools Track a Package Informed Delivery …

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has children which are not allowed: li[tabindex]
`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent)

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Disabilities affected: ~15.9K ~4.8K ~35.1K

Affected elements (10):

Element 1

Element path: nav > ul.nav-list > li.qt-nav > a.nav-first-element

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parents role not present: menu, menubar, group

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~15.9K ~4.8K ~35.1K

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~15.9K ~4.8K ~74.9K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~15.9K ~4.8K ~35.1K

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~15.7K ~4.7K ~34.5K

Affected elements (4):

Element 1

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li.active

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="0" aria-checked="true" class="active">

Label: Slide 1

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="true"

Element 2

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="1" aria-checked="false">

Label: Slide 2

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"

Element 3

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="2" aria-checked="false">

Label: Slide 3

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"

Element 4

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="3" aria-checked="false">

Label: Slide 4

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"
Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children)

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Disabilities affected: ~15.7K ~4.7K ~34.5K

Element path: div#g-navigation > div.nav-full > nav > ul.nav-list

Label: Skip Quick Tools Links Quick Tools Track a Package Informed Delivery …

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has children which are not allowed: li[tabindex]
`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent)

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Disabilities affected: ~15.7K ~4.7K ~34.5K

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~15.7K ~4.7K

Element 1

Element path: body > img

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport)

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~11.8K ~3.6K ~55.7K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~11.8K ~3.6K

Affected elements (4):

Element 1

Element path: div.five-sixth-last > div#topnews > div.icon > img

Snippet:

<img src="/bundles/templating/images/top_news/important.png">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#us

Snippet:

<img id="us" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/US.png" usemap="#US_large_imagemap_800_500">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 3

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#alaska

Snippet:

<img id="alaska" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/ak.png" usemap="#alaska_imagemap_130_115">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 4

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#hawaii

Snippet:

<img id="hawaii" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/hi.png" usemap="#hawaii_imagemap">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~26.1K ~68.7K

Affected elements (53):

Element 1

Element path: div#forecast-lookup > form#getForecast > div#txtHelp > a

Snippet:

<a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="javascript:void(window.open('https://www.weather.gov/ForecastSearchHelp.ht…">

Label: Location Help

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (81.5px by 15px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 15px instead of at least 24px.

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~6.5K ~2.0K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div > div.blazy > div.media > img.media__element

Snippet:

<img decoding="async" class="media__element b-lazy" loading="lazy" src="" width="480" height="360">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch)

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Disabilities affected: ~6.5K ~15.7K ~2.0K ~14.4K

Affected elements (6):

Element 1

Element path: div.usa-nav-container > div.region > div#logo > a.usa-logo__text

  • Text inside the element is not included in the accessible name

Accessibility Findings

https://travel.state.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 93

Axe findings: 2

`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport)

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~13.6K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

Accessibility Findings

https://cdc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 3

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~4.9K ~1.5K ~10.8K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: nav.mobile-navbar > div.mobile-navbar_top > div.d-flex > a#cdc-mobile-search-toggle

Snippet:

<a id="cdc-mobile-search-toggle" class="nav-link" href="#" aria-controls="cdc-mobile-search-form">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Skip links are not focusable. (rule: skip-link)

Including a skip link can help users skip to the main content to save time. Learn more about skip links.

Disabilities affected: ~4.9K ~1.5K ~10.8K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#headerSearch" tabindex="2">

Label: Skip to search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • No skip link target
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~4.9K ~1.5K ~10.8K

Affected elements (3):

Element 1

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#content" tabindex="1">

Label: Skip to site content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 2

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#headerSearch" tabindex="2">

Label: Skip to search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 3

Element path: div#hero-carousel > div.carousel-indicators > div.carousel-indicators-wrapper > button#animation-toggle

Snippet:

<button id="animation-toggle" aria-pressed="true" tabindex="3">

Label: Pause carousel

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~10.0K ~17.9K

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.fsa-banner-content-body > span > div > a

Snippet:

Label: visit StudentAid.gov/courtactions

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.64:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #0000ee, surrounding text: #212529)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element path: div.fsa-banner-content-body > span > div > a

Label: Log in

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

https://science.nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 95

Axe findings: 1

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~3.9K ~1.2K

Affected elements (4):

Element 1

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button#global-navigation-trigger--mobile

Snippet:

<button id="global-navigation-trigger--mobile" class="hds-global-menu-toggle menu-toggle menu-mobile-toggle" aria-expanded="false" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="left">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Accessibility Findings

https://radar.weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 77

Axe findings: 2

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~3.6K ~1.1K ~16.9K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport)

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~8.6K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=0">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

https://gsaadvantage.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 84

Axe findings: 6

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~3.2K ~950 ~7.0K

https://uscis.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 96

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children)

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

https://nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 2

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~2.7K ~798

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button#global-navigation-trigger--mobile

  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Accessibility Findings

https://usitc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 95

Axe findings: 3

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus)

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Disabilities affected: ~2.6K ~6.2K ~780 ~5.7K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: body > div.dialog-off-canvas-main-canvas > section#search-dropdown

Snippet:

<section aria-label="Search box" id="search-dropdown" aria-hidden="true" class="search-dropdown desktop:display-none">

Label: Search

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should be disabled or be removed from the DOM
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~2.6K ~780 ~5.7K

`<frame>` or `<iframe>` elements do not have a title (rule: frame-title)

Screen reader users rely on frame titles to describe the contents of frames. Learn more about frame titles.

Disabilities affected: ~2.6K ~775 ~5.7K

  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~2.6K ~775 ~12.1K

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~2.6K ~775 ~5.7K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="//www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark
`<td>` elements in a large `<table>` do not have table headers. (rule: td-has-header)

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that `<td>` elements in a large table (3 or more cells in width and height) have an associated table header may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about table headers.

Disabilities affected: ~2.6K ~775

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: tbody > tr > td > table

Snippet:

<table cellspacing="1" cellpadding="2" border="0" width="100%">

Label: Hazard Mon (03/16) Tue (03/17) Wed (03/18) Thu (03/19) Fri (03/20) Sat (03/21) …

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Some non-empty data cells do not have table headers

Accessibility Findings

https://fas.usda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 97

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus)

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Disabilities affected: ~2.6K ~6.2K ~770 ~5.6K

Affected elements (6):

Element 1

Element path: div#tns1-mw > div#tns1-iw > div#tns1 > div#tns1-item1

Snippet:

<div class="c-heroslider__slide tns-item" id="tns1-item1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1">

Label: Why Do Ag Exports Matter to U.S. Farmers and the U.S. Economy? Learn More

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM

Element 2

Element path: div.c-slickslider__slides > div.slick-list > div.slick-track > div.c-card

<div class="c-card slick-slide" style="width: 292px; height: 304.766px;" data-slick-index="1" aria-hidden="true">

Label: March 3, 2026 Export Sales Announcement Export Sales to Unknown Destinations Pr…

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM

Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch)

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Disabilities affected: ~2.6K ~6.2K ~770 ~5.6K

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.c-heroslider__nav > div.l-constrain > div.c-heroslider__nav-inner > button.tns-nav-active

Snippet:

<button data-nav="0" aria-label="Carousel Page 1 (Current Slide)" aria-controls="tns1" class="tns-nav-active">

Label: Deputy Under Secretary Bekkering Advances Administration’s America First Agenda…

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Text inside the element is not included in the accessible name

Element 2

Element path: div.c-heroslider__nav > div.l-constrain > div.c-heroslider__nav-inner > button

Snippet:

<button data-nav="1" tabindex="-1" aria-label="Carousel Page 2" aria-controls="tns1">

Label: Why Do Ag Exports Matter to U.S. Farmers and the U.S. Economy?

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Text inside the element is not included in the accessible name

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://ecfr.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 73

Axe findings: 7

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~2.5K ~740 ~5.4K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.row > div.col-xs-12 > div > div#suggestions

Snippet:

<div id="suggestions" data-controller="suggestions autocomplete" data-autocomplete-url-value="/suggestions" data-autocomplete-delay-value="300" aria-expanded="false" data-autocomplete-ready-value="true">

Label:

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~2.5K ~740

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: form.simple_form > div.input-group > span.input-group-btn > button.btn

Snippet:

<button class="btn btn-optional" type="button" data-suggestions-target="nonModalSearchIcon" data-action="click-&gt;placeholder#suggestions">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~5.9K ~10.6K

Affected elements (23):

Element 1

Element path: div#main-navigation > div.user-utils > div.signed-out > a.utils-signed-out

Snippet:

<a class="utils-signed-out" tabindex="-1" data-turbo="false" href="https://www.ecfr.gov/auth/sign_in?jwt=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJub3RpZmljYXR…">

Label: Sign In / Sign Up

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.63 (foreground color: #428bca, background color: #ffffff, font size: 11.3pt (15px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~5.9K ~10.6K

  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 93

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~2.4K ~707 ~5.2K

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.th-wrapper > div#earthdata-tophat2 > div.th-link-container > a#th-help-link

Snippet:

<a href="javascript:void(0);" id="th-help-link" class="th-right-panel-link">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 2

Element path: div.th-wrapper > div#earthdata-tophat2 > div#th-status-link > a.th-right-panel-link

Snippet:

<a href="javascript:void(0);" class="th-right-panel-link th-status-link">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 2

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~2.2K ~659

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: aside#tsp-toolbar > ul > li > button#toggle-tsp-zoom

Snippet:

<button id="toggle-tsp-zoom" class="tooltip">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list)

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

Disabilities affected: ~2.1K ~634

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.container > div.row > div.col-xs-12 > ul

Snippet:

<ul _ngcontent-ng-c2940030545="">

Label: About ESTA and The Visa Waiver Program What is the difference between an ESTA …

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • List element has direct children that are not allowed: div
List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Disabilities affected: ~1.9K ~584 ~4.3K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.fda-ft-container > div.fda-ft-heading > div.fda-ft-heading-text > h3.fda-ft-heading-title

Snippet:

<h3 class="fda-ft-heading-title">

Label: Featured Topics

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1.9K ~584 ~4.3K

Affected elements (3):

Element 1

Element path: div#quicklinks > ul.list-inline > li > a.btn

Snippet:

<a href="#main-content" class="btn btn-default margin-top-3" tabindex="1" data-once="download-role-button lang-fix-multilingual">

Label: Skip to main content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 2

Element path: div#quicklinks > ul.list-inline > li > a.btn

Snippet:

<a href="#search-form" class="btn btn-default margin-top-3" tabindex="1" data-once="download-role-button lang-fix-multilingual">

Label: Skip to FDA Search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 3

Element path: div#quicklinks > ul.list-inline > li > a.btn

Snippet:

<a href="#footer" class="btn btn-default margin-top-3" tabindex="1" data-once="download-role-button lang-fix-multilingual">

Label: Skip to footer links

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Accessibility Findings

https://ncei.noaa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 98

Axe findings: 2

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~1.7K ~508 ~3.7K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html.js

Snippet:

<html lang="en" dir="ltr" prefix="content: http://purl.org/rss/1.0/modules/content/  dc: http://purl.org/dc/…" class="js">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark
Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch)

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Disabilities affected: ~1.7K ~4.1K ~508 ~3.7K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: header > nav.navbar > div.container > button.navbar-toggler

Snippet:

<button class="navbar-toggler" type="button" data-toggle="collapse" data-target=".navbar-collapse" aria-controls="navbar-collapse" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation">

Label: MENU

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Text inside the element is not included in the accessible name

Accessibility Findings

https://mynasadata.larc.nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 87

Axe findings: 3

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~1.7K ~502 ~3.7K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.menu-container > div.mobile-banner > div#mobile-icon-button > a#mainmenu

Snippet:

<a data-toggle="collapse" data-target="#mobile-menus" id="mainmenu" class="btn btn-navbar" aria-expanded="true">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="true"
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1.7K ~502 ~3.7K

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.mnd-hp-gradient-wrapper > div.mnd-content-wrapper > div.mnd-content-container > a

Snippet:

<a href="../basic-page/earth-system-data-explorer" target="_blank" aria-describedby="new-window-0">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 2

Element path: div.mnd-content-wrapper > div.mnd-content-container > div.mnd-hp-content > a

Snippet:

<a href="../basic-page/earth-system-data-explorer" target="_blank" aria-describedby="new-window-0">

Label:

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~3.7K ~9.7K

Affected elements (6):

Element 1

Element path: div.ft-links > div.icon_container > span > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.youtube.com/user/MyNASAData" target="_blank" aria-describedby="new-window-0">

Label: My Nasa Data Youtube Channel

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (68px by 14px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 0px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2

Element path: div.icon_container > span > a > img.social-icon-size

Snippet:

<img src="/themes/mynasadata_theme/img/youtube_icon_red_and_black.svg" tabindex="0" alt="My Nasa Data Youtube Channel" class="social-icon-size footer-youtube-icon" role="button" aria-label="youtube icon">

Label: My Nasa Data Youtube Channel

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (58px by 13.1px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 0px instead of at least 24px.

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 10.2px instead of at least 24px.

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 10.2px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 70

Axe findings: 9

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children)

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Disabilities affected: ~1.5K ~460 ~3.4K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div#g-navigation > div.nav-full > nav > ul.nav-list

Snippet:

<ul class="nav-list" role="menubar">

Label: Skip Quick Tools Links Quick Tools Track a Package Informed Delivery …

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has children which are not allowed: li[tabindex]
`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent)

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Disabilities affected: ~1.5K ~460 ~3.4K

Affected elements (10):

Element 1

Element path: nav > ul.nav-list > li.qt-nav > a.nav-first-element

Snippet:

<a aria-expanded="false" role="menuitem" tabindex="0" aria-haspopup="true" class="nav-first-element menuitem" href="#">

Label: Quick Tools

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parents role not present: menu, menubar, group

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~3.4K ~8.9K

Affected elements (11):

Element 1

Element path: li.global-footer--navigation-category > ol.global-footer--navigation-options > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://about.usps.com/newsroom/service-alerts/">

Label: USPS Service Updates

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (116.4px by 13px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16.8px instead of at least 24px.

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~3.7K ~6.6K

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div#content > div.toolbar > div.search-helper > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://docs.ckan.org/en/2.10/user-guide.html?#search-in-detail" target="_blank">

Label: "Search in Detail" instructions.

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.92:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #284a7a, surrounding text: #1b1b1b)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 2

Element path: div.dataset-content > div.notes > div > a

Snippet:

<a href="http://on.ny.gov/1GpWiHD" rel="noopener">

Label: http://on.ny.gov/1GpWiHD

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.92:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #284a7a, surrounding text: #1b1b1b)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1.5K ~459 ~3.4K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.toolbar > ol.breadcrumb > li.home > a

Snippet:

<a href="/dataset/">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list)

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

Disabilities affected: ~1.5K ~459

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.primary > section.module > div.module-content > ul.dataset-list

Snippet:

<ul class="dataset-list unstyled">

Label: Federal Authorizations From 10/01/2006 Thru 09/30/2025 Export-Import Bank of …

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • List element has direct children that are not allowed: strong
List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Accessibility Findings

https://medicare.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 97

Axe findings: 1

`<frame>` or `<iframe>` elements do not have a title (rule: frame-title)

Screen reader users rely on frame titles to describe the contents of frames. Learn more about frame titles.

Disabilities affected: ~1.5K ~459 ~3.4K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.QSIWebResponsive-creative-container-fade > div.QSIWebResponsiveDialog-Layout1-SI_6tbIx8xW9ShoDfU_content > div.QSIWebResponsiveDialog-Layout1-SI_6tbIx8xW9ShoDfU_embedded-target-container > iframe

Snippet:

<iframe class="" data-interceptid="SI_6tbIx8xW9ShoDfU" name="survey-iframe-SI_6tbIx8xW9ShoDfU" data-name="survey-iframe-SI_6tbIx8xW9ShoDfU" tabindex="0" style="width: 100%; height: 100%; border: none; position: absolute; top: 0px;">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has no title attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://my.uscis.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 89

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~1.5K ~447 ~3.3K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.uscis-row > div.col-span-6 > div.mobile-header > a#toggle-sections

Snippet:

<a class="hamburger hamburger--squeeze" tabindex="0" type="button" id="toggle-sections" aria-expanded="false">

Label: Menu

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport)

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~3.6K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,height=device-height,user-scalable=no,initial-scale=1.0…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1.5K ~445 ~7.0K

https://urs.earthdata.nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 81

Axe findings: 5

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1.3K ~399

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div#tophat3Widget > nav#tophat3WidgetNavBar > div.container-fluid > button#tophat3HamburgerButton

Snippet:

<button class="navbar-toggler" type="button" id="tophat3HamburgerButton">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1.3K ~399

  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~3.2K ~5.7K

Element path: body.static_pages > div.container > div.home-info-box > a

  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

`<td>` elements in a large `<table>` do not have table headers. (rule: td-has-header)

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that `<td>` elements in a large table (3 or more cells in width and height) have an associated table header may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about table headers.

Accessibility Findings

https://aviationweather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 92

Axe findings: 3

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~2.7K ~4.9K

Affected elements (4):

Element 1

Element path: div.container > div.carousel-item > div.d-block > h4

Snippet:

<h4>

Label: Welcome!

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 4.44 (foreground color: #f8f9fa, background color: #6c757d, font size: 16.2pt (21.636px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Element 2

Element path: div.container > div.carousel-item > div.d-block > span.align-middle

<span class="align-middle">

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~1.1K ~340 ~2.5K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.card > div.card-body > a.btn > h4

Snippet:

<h4>

Label: Expanded Archive

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1.1K ~340 ~2.5K

Affected elements (4):

Element 1

Element path: nav#main-navbar > ul.navbar-nav > li.nav-item > a#dropdown-weather

Snippet:

<a class="nav-link dropdown-toggle" href="#" id="dropdown-weather" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded="false">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~2.5K ~6.5K

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div#footer-menus > ul#menu--footer-2 > li.menu__item > a.menu__link

Snippet:

<a href="/protecting-your-privacy" class="menu__link" data-ga="Footer:Protecting Your Privacy" data-drupal-link-system-path="node/5778">

Label: Protecting Your Privacy

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (158.8px by 22px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 22px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2

Element path: div#footer-menus > ul#menu--footer-2 > li.menu__item > a.menu__link

<a href="https://vote.gov/" class="menu__link" data-ga="Footer:Vote.gov">

  • Target has insufficient size (60.4px by 22px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 22px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://fdic.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 82

Axe findings: 5

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~1.1K ~321 ~2.4K

Affected elements (3):

Element 1

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.tabs-container > input#consumers

Snippet:

<input class="radiotab" name="tabs" type="radio" id="consumers" checked="checked" aria-expanded="false" data-once="tab-click">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"

Element 2

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.tabs-container > input#bankers

Snippet:

<input class="radiotab" name="tabs" type="radio" id="bankers" aria-expanded="false" data-once="tab-click">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"

Element 3

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.tabs-container > input#analysts

Snippet:

<input class="radiotab" name="tabs" type="radio" id="analysts" aria-expanded="false" data-once="tab-click">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1.1K ~321

Affected elements (10):

Element 1

Element path: div.region > div#block-fdic-theme-mainnavigation > nav#tbm-main > button.tbm-button

Snippet:

<button class="tbm-button" type="button">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

`<frame>` or `<iframe>` elements do not have a title (rule: frame-title)

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1.1K ~321 ~2.4K

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: body.acquia-cms-toolbar > a.skipheader

Snippet:

<a href="#main-content" class="skipheader" tabindex="1">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element path: div.usa-banner__inner > div.usa-banner__header > div > button#fdic_how_you_know

Snippet:

<button class="usa-banner__header-button" tabindex="1" id="fdic_how_you_know" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="gov-banner" aria-label="Here’s how you know" data-once="govBanner">

Label: Here’s how you know

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~1.0K ~302 ~2.2K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.container > div.row > div.col-xl-4 > h5

Snippet:

<h5 translate="">

Label: CONTACT SUPPORT

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~2.4K ~4.3K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.col-12 > div.row > div.col-12 > a.enrollment-link

Snippet:

<a data-bs-target="#mdl-what-i-need" data-bs-toggle="modal" href="" translate="" class="enrollment-link">

Label: What Information do I need?

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 2:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #00568f, surrounding text: #212529)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Accessibility Findings

https://fbi.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 79

Axe findings: 5

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~1.0K ~301 ~2.2K

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.g-4 > div.col-6 > div.quick-link > h6

Snippet:

<h6>

Label: Apply for an FBI Job

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid

Element 2

Element path: div.container > div.row > div.col-md-3 > h5.mb-3

Snippet:

<h5 class="mb-3">

Label: Most Wanted

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1.0K ~301

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.top-nav-bar > div.top-nav-bar-bottom > div.left > img

Snippet:

<img src="https://www.fbi.gov/++theme++fbigov.theme/uswds-2.9.0/img/icon-dot-gov.svg">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2

Element path: div.top-nav-bar > div.top-nav-bar-bottom > div.right > img

Snippet:

<img src="https://www.fbi.gov/++theme++fbigov.theme/uswds-2.9.0/img/icon-https.svg">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1.0K ~301 ~2.2K

Affected elements (8):

Element 1

Element path: div.container > div.header-mobile-row > div > a.nav-link

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.fbi.gov/++theme++2025-05-16/index.html" class="nav-link px-3 py-2" data-bs-toggle="offcanvas" data-bs-target="#mobileNav" aria-controls="mobileNav">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty

  • Element has no title attribute

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~2.2K ~5.8K

Affected elements (4):

Element 1

Element path: section.hero-section > div.hero-indicators > div.d-flex > button.indicator

Snippet:

<button class="indicator active" aria-label="Slide 1" data-slide="1">

Label: Slide 1

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (12px by 12px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2

Element path: section.hero-section > div.hero-indicators > div.d-flex > button.indicator

Snippet:

<button class="indicator" aria-label="Slide 2" data-slide="2">

Label: Slide 2

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (12px by 12px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

Element 3

Element path: section.hero-section > div.hero-indicators > div.d-flex > button.indicator

Snippet:

<button class="indicator" aria-label="Slide 3" data-slide="3">

Label: Slide 3

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (12px by 12px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

Element 4

Element path: section.hero-section > div.hero-indicators > div.d-flex > button.indicator

Snippet:

<button class="indicator" aria-label="Slide 4" data-slide="4">

Label: Slide 4

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (12px by 12px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.
Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~1.0K ~301 ~2.2K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html lang="en" data-gridsystem="bs3">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark

Accessibility Findings

https://marine.weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 67

Axe findings: 5

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~2.3K ~4.2K

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.one-sixth-first > div#forecast-lookup > form#getForecast > input#inputstring

Snippet:

<input id="inputstring" name="inputstring" type="text" value="Enter location ..." onclick="this.value=''" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.84 (foreground color: #999999, background color: #ffffff, font size: 8.0pt (10.6667px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Element 2

Element path: div#topnews > div.body > p > a

Snippet:

<a href="http://www.wpc.ncep.noaa.gov/discussions/hpcdiscussions.php?disc=pmdspd" target="_blank">

Label: Read More >

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 4.07 (foreground color: #1763ab, background color: #d2d2d2, font size: 10.0pt (13.3333px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~973 ~292 ~4.6K

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
